summary: Matched `mnEvent_8024CE74` by restoring a `static inline` helper, `mnEvent_CountUnlocked`, that contains the unlock-counting loop. The PR body says this recovered a 100% match by making MWCC emit a register-copy initialization shape instead of two independent zero loads. Review feedback rejected unrelated data/assert-literal work and recommended landing only the focused code match.

key_files.role: Final changed file; adds `mnEvent_CountUnlocked()` static inline wrapper and changes `mnEvent_8024CE74()` to call it after the debug-level early return.
Reviewed earlier or non-final hunk involving local `HSD_ASSERT` override around `jobj.h`; reviewer called it premature sdata work. Not listed in final changed-file metadata.
Reviewed earlier or non-final hunk declaring static literal symbols; reviewer said these literals did not need declarations. Not listed in final changed-file metadata.

what_changed.text: Replaced the inline loop body in `mnEvent_8024CE74` with a call to a new `static inline s32 mnEvent_CountUnlocked(void)` helper.
Moved the `for (i = 0; i < 0x33; i++)` count loop into `mnEvent_CountUnlocked`, using `s32 i`, `s32 count = 0`, and `gmMainLib_8015CEFC(i) != 0` before returning `count`.
Removed the prior `PAD_STACK(8)` from `mnEvent_8024CE74` in the shown diff.
Kept the `g_debugLevel > 2` early return in `mnEvent_8024CE74`, then assigns `count = mnEvent_CountUnlocked()` before the existing `count <= 5` branch.

smart_moves.text: Used a small static inline helper to preserve readable source while steering MWCC toward the expected inlined register dependency.
Grounded the helper shape in nearby menu patterns named in the PR body: `mnInfo_CountUnlocked` and `mnCount_CountUnlockedChars`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Same pattern as mnInfo_CountUnlocked / mnCount_CountUnlockedChars

smart_moves.text: Final diff stayed focused on the code match in `mnevent.c` after review objected to unrelated data/assert-literal changes.

decomp_lessons.lesson: A local `static inline` wrapper can be a plausible authored-source reconstruction and a codegen tactic when MWCC needs locals declared together inside the inlined callee to produce a data-dependent register copy.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: expected build uses addi r30, r31, 0
human_pr_text: only happens when both locals are defined together inside a static inline wrapper

decomp_lessons.lesson: When matching a function, compare against nearby matched sibling helpers before keeping a flattened loop directly in the caller.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Same pattern as mnInfo_CountUnlocked / mnCount_CountUnlockedChars elsewhere in mn/

decomp_lessons.lesson: Remove narrow stack-padding tactics when the underlying source shape explains the stack/register behavior.

naming_conventions.text: The restored helper is named with the local subsystem prefix and action pattern: `mnEvent_CountUnlocked`, matching the PR-cited sibling names `mnInfo_CountUnlocked` and `mnCount_CountUnlockedChars`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Same pattern as mnInfo_CountUnlocked / mnCount_CountUnlockedChars

naming_conventions.text: Use simple role locals such as `i` and `count` for a small count loop when their roles are evident.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Recover a static inline wrapper so MWCC inlines the helper but keeps the helper's local initialization relationship, yielding `addi r30, r31, 0` instead of a second `li`.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: li r31/li r30 vs expected addi r30,r31,0
human_pr_text: only happens when both locals are defined together inside a static inline wrapper

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Prefer `gmMainLib_8015CEFC(i) != 0` in the helper condition for the matched source shape shown in the PR and diff.

Use build/report evidence to confirm a small codegen change: PR body claims `python configure.py && ninja` green, `mnEvent_8024CE74` 99.4% to 100%, and no other functions affected; bot report records one new match at 100.00%.

review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer objected to a local `HSD_ASSERT` override around `jobj.h` in `src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.c`, calling it slop and, at best, premature sdata work.
review_feedback.author: ribbanya

review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer objected to declaring `mnNameNew_804D4F84` and `mnNameNew_804D4F8C` in `src/melee/mn/mnnamenew.h`, saying they are static literals and do not need to be declared anywhere.
review_feedback.author: ribbanya

review_feedback.feedback: Reviewer recommended merging only the `mnEvent_8024CE74` match and not worrying about data-section matching in this PR; data sections for a TU can be matched after code is matched.
review_feedback.author: ribbanya

follow_up_queries: Find other mn/ count helpers that match the `mn*_CountUnlocked` static inline pattern.
Search for remaining `PAD_STACK` uses in small count loops where an inline helper may recover MWCC register-copy initialization.
Search review history for local `HSD_ASSERT` override attempts around `jobj.h` and static literal declarations.
Search for string-literal or static-literal declarations added only for sdata or data-section matching in code-match PRs.

notes: Final changed-file metadata lists only `src/melee/mn/mnevent.c`; review comments reference `mnnamenew.c` and `mnnamenew.h`, so those are treated as reviewed earlier or non-final hunks rather than final changed files.
Verification claims are recorded from the PR body and bot report; no independent build or objdiff was run during intake.
The PR body states the helper was removed in #2447, but this intake did not inspect #2447.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Static inline wrapper can recover MWCC local-initialization dependency
accepted_candidate_records.text: In `mnEvent_8024CE74`, moving a count loop into `static inline s32 mnEvent_CountUnlocked(void)` made MWCC inline the helper while preserving a local initialization relationship that produced the expected `addi r30, r31, 0` instead of two independent `li` zero loads. Consider sibling local patterns before flattening such loops into the caller.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep focused code matches separate from speculative data-section/literal cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: For this PR, review requested landing only the `mnEvent_8024CE74` code match and rejected unrelated `mnnamenew` assert/literal declarations as premature sdata work.

source_update_candidates.title: Inline helper recovery as an MWCC matching tactic
source_update_candidates.text: When a small repeated/counting operation appears flattened in a caller, check nearby matched siblings for a `static inline` helper pattern. In PR #2510, restoring `mnEvent_CountUnlocked` made MWCC emit the expected register-copy initialization and removed the need for `PAD_STACK`.
source_update_candidates.reason: This may extend existing standards on inferred authored style, natural loops, and evidence-backed matching tactics with a concrete static-inline helper pattern.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not override assert macros or declare static literals just to chase data sections in a focused code-match PR
source_update_candidates.text: Review feedback in PR #2510 rejected local `HSD_ASSERT` override/sdata work and declarations for static literals, recommending that the PR merge only the focused code match and leave TU data-section matching for later human cleanup.
source_update_candidates.reason: This reinforces existing standards on text-before-data matching, literal ownership, and assert macro cleanup with explicit review evidence.

rejection_notes: Do not promote `mnEvent_CountUnlocked` as a universal required shape for all count loops; the evidence is specific to `mnEvent_8024CE74` and PR-cited mn sibling patterns.
Do not treat the reviewer comments on `mnnamenew.c` and `mnnamenew.h` as final changed files, because final changed-file metadata lists only `mnevent.c`.
Do not independently claim that #2447 removed the helper without inspecting PR #2447; record only that PR #2510's body says so.
